markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Prepare replacement text for stale SQLite/file-authority passages so WP-009 aligns with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. This MT belongs to MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed. Prepare the future Master Spec refinement/enrichment payload without relying on sidecar research.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Prepare spec-enrichment-ready wording without editing the Master Spec in this stub task.
Where current Master Spec text is stale, mark the exact stale contract and the replacement Handshake-native rule.
scope.expected_behavior: PostgresEventLedgerSpecPatch leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PostgresEventLedgerSpecPatch is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: AC1 (PostgresEventLedgerSpecPatch specified for no-context continuation) -> the replacement text was not only prepared but applied: .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/11-shared-dev-platform-and-oss-foundations.md#9.1.1 WP-KERNEL-009 authority supersession (older SQLite-compatible roadmap and fallback language superseded; PostgreSQL/EventLedger is the only authority path; SQLite references are legacy research context or unrelated product debt, not acceptable authority/cache/offline/compatibility/bootstrap/test/proof paths) and 02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11 (SQLite, embedded local relational stores, browser storage, generated wiki folders, Docker-only proof, external app state, provider chat history MUST NOT satisfy ProjectKnowledgeIndex authority, replay, validation, cache or fixture requirements); stale source passages enumerated in refinement.json spec_conflicts_requiring_enrichment SPEC-CONFLICT-001 and spec-enrichment-proposal.json module_change_plan stale_conflict_examples
Patch applied with hashes -> .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-changelog.jsonl v02.192 entry records before/after module sha256 for 02 and 11 and validation PASS results

AC2 (exact files, state tables, constraints, failure modes, verification evidence) -> exact files pinned in this MT scope.relevant_files and scope.constraints plus the line anchors above; failure modes: stub failure_scenarios (implementation tries to introduce SQLite or a second durable database, mitigated by no-SQLite tripwire); verification evidence: refinement.json refinement.verification_results (4x PASS, 1x BLOCKED_PRE_EXISTING)

AC3 (sidecar research rejected, ideas restated Handshake-native) -> stub research_basis.normative_status=non_normative_context_only + translation_rule; replacement text expressed as Handshake product law; this MT scope.constraints bar sidecar-paper and outside-runtime proof

AC4 (HBR rows named with concrete reasons) -> HBR-INT-001, HBR-MAN-001, HBR-SWARM-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE for runtime proof on this spec-seed record MT (no product surface, code, test, or runtime state changed by it; requirement content recorded for implementation MTs in .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7, 10-product-surfaces.md#10.15.8 and #10.20); CX-503R PostgreSQL-only storage law = SATISFIED at spec level by 02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11 and 11-shared-dev-platform-and-oss-foundations.md#9.1.1 which lock PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only authority and bar SQLite for WP-KERNEL-009 paths; per packet.json acceptance_matrix.note HBR proof is carried per-MT

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.

Verified 9.1.1 supersession wording and the 2.3.13.11 MUST-NOT storage list verbatim, SPEC-CONFLICT-001 and proposal stale_conflict_examples line anchors (7 of 11 literal SQLite hits, 4 contextual), and changelog before/after sha256 for modules 02 and 11.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
